codeReview.summary: The fix addresses the prior blocking gap by keeping saved Riot ID lookups synchronized with localStorage after successful lookup mutations, so the dashboard's recent-account state rehydrates correctly after deep-link selection, saved-lookup reselection, filter changes, and refresh while preserving the existing route-precedence and history-preference behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The web-feature-worker skill still tells workers to write failing tests first when possible, but the original history-filters worker handoff/tests show the feature landed with new helper coverage added after implementation rather than as an initial failing test. The skill could be clearer that persisted-state fixes may instead rely on targeted post-implementation regression tests when touching the monolithic dashboard flow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Original feature diff 573ba22 adds lib/history-preferences.test.ts alongside the new helper/UI code, and the fix handoff for worker session 99e4e5c-902a-4f57-95dd-c7065e8cf38d explicitly suggests clarifying storage-backed persisted-state guidance in .factory/skills/web-feature-worker/SKILL.md.

summary: Reviewed the prior failed review, both the original feature commit (573ba22) and the fix commit (98d4587), the fix handoff, transcript skeleton, and shared-state docs. The new saved-lookup subscription closes the original VAL-CROSS-002 gap by rehydrating recent Riot IDs from storage after successful lookup mutations, and I found no remaining blocking issues in the scoped filter/preferences/reset behavior.
